category_title: First-grade struggling readers who prioritize diction accuracy over composition through persistent repetition of single phrases
scenario_title: Persistent diction testing during patterned composition on iPad
minimal_context: Alyson is using speech recognition software on an iPad in the writing center to dictate her version of Mary Had a Little Lamb.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's work on your version of Mary Had a Little Lamb. Go ahead and dictate your first sentence into the iPad.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Whispers to herself several times) Carla had a baby pit bull. (Launches SR and dictates) Carla had a baby pit bull.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, let's look at what the iPad wrote down.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: That’s not what I said! It says 'Carla had hey baby pit bull.' (Deletes the entire sentence)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Deletes the incorrect text) a baby pit bull. (Deletes the text again) a baby pit bull. (Continues deleting and re-dictating the same phrase 10 to 15 times)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's work on your version of Mary Had a Little Lamb. Go ahead and dictate your first sentence into the iPad.

STUDENT: (Whispers to herself several times) Carla had a baby pit bull. (Launches SR and dictates) Carla had a baby pit bull.

TUTOR: Okay, let's look at what the iPad wrote down.

STUDENT: That’s not what I said! It says 'Carla had hey baby pit bull.' (Deletes the entire sentence)

TUTOR: Just try dictating it again.

STUDENT: (Deletes the incorrect text) a baby pit bull. (Deletes the text again) a baby pit bull. (Continues deleting and re-dictating the same phrase 10 to 15 times)

task.content: Alyson worked on her version of Mary Had a Little Lamb by dictating, 'Carla had a baby pit bull.'

context.materials: iPad, speech recognition (SR) software, chart paper
context.social_setting: working independently with teacher available for assistance

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: SR consistently wrote, 'Carla had hey baby pit bull.'
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Ten to 15 times, Alyson deleted the incorrect text and dictated, 'a baby pit bull.' Students commonly tested their diction instead of composing stories.
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: She wanted to see SR transcribe what she said; she wanted her diction to be recognized.

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: they deleted entire sentences and drafts
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Students wanted to skip brainstorming and drafting and go straight to revising.

behavior_patterns.condition: When participants were unsure of the appropriateness of a transcribed word
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: they consistently and quickly turned to me for assistance

behavior_patterns.condition: As she brainstormed her version of the anonymous poem
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: She whispered this phrase to herself several times as she launched SR
related_quotes: Five little kittens sat by the pond
